title: digitaria pruriens
intro: Digitaria pruriens, also known as Dhamna or Cheewra, is a plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in India. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Digitaria pruriens include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in digestive problems. The plant is often prepared as an herbal decoction or powder for consumption, and it can also be made into a paste or oil for topical application.
